title: त्रिपुरसुन्दरी पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री त्रिपुरसुन्दरी पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

श्रीमद्राजराजेश्वर्यै नमः परापररूपिण्यै।
कामेश्वरकामिन्यै नमो ललितायै चिदम्बरम्॥ १॥

बालार्कबिम्बसंकाशां चतुर्बाहुसमन्विताम्।
पाशाङ्कुशधनुर्बाणधारिणीं ललितां भजे॥ २॥

श्रीचक्रराजनिलयां बिन्दुमध्यनिवासिनीम्।
षोडशीं महाविद्यां श्रीविद्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

त्रिपुरां त्रिपुरेशीं च त्रिपुरासुन्दरीं शिवाम्।
परमानन्दरूपां तां ललिताम्बां नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्यां सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनीम्।
सर्वसौभाग्यजनन्तीं ललिताम्बिकां नमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री त्रिपुरसुन्दरी पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
benefits: Sacred Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam for devotion and spiritual growth
Invokes blessings of Tripurasundari in all forms
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and bestows spiritual merit
Ideal for Tripurasundari festivals and daily worship
description: The Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam, known in Sanskrit as त्रिपुरसुन्दरी पञ्चरत्नम्, is five gems of devotional verses in praise of Goddess Parvati. Attributed to Lalita Sahasranama Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to remove sins and bestow spiritual merit, grant divine protection and auspiciousness, and invoke blessings of Tripurasundari in all forms. It is especially recommended on Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Lalita Panchami for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 5 verses, it can be completed in approximately 3 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam (त्रिपुरसुन्दरी पञ्चरत्नम्) is a five-verse (pancharatnam = five jewels) Sanskrit devotional hymn addressed to Devi Tripura Sundari — also known as Lalita, Rajarajeshwari, Shodashi, Kameshwari, Sri Vidya — the supreme goddess of the Sri Vidya tradition and one of the ten Mahavidyas. The five verses systematically invoke her as the consort of Kameshwara Shiva, the four-armed wielder of the noose, goad, sugarcane bow and flower arrows, the goddess seated in the bindu of the Sri Chakra, the sixteen-syllabled Shodashi mantra, the supreme Tripura (ruling the three cities, three states, three gunas) and the bestower of all auspiciousness, success and prosperity. It is recited in Sri Vidya upasana, on Fridays, during Lalita Jayanti and Sharada Navaratri. (Source: classical Sri Vidya stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Devi Tripura Sundari and what does she represent?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Tripura Sundari is the supreme goddess of the Sri Vidya tradition — Lalita Mahatripura Sundari — described in the Brahmanda Purana's Lalita Mahatmya, the Lalita Sahasranama, the Soundarya Lahari of Adi Shankara, and the Tripura Upanishad. Her name means the beautiful one of the three cities — the goddess who pervades and rules the three worlds (svarga, martya, patala), the three states of consciousness (jagrat, svapna, sushupti), the three gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas), the three deities (Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra) and the three letters of pranava (a-u-m). She is the personification of supreme beauty (sundari), the bindu at the centre of the Sri Chakra, the consort of Kameshwara, and the source of the entire universe. She is worshipped as the Tripura, the Shodashi, and the Rajarajeshwari (queen of queens). (Sources: Brahmanda Purana Lalita Mahatmya; Lalita Sahasranama; Soundarya Lahari.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam is most powerful on Friday (Shukravar — sacred to the goddess), during the Lalita Jayanti (Magha Purnima), throughout Sharada Navaratri (especially the eighth day Durga Ashtami, and the Lalita Panchami of Ashvin Shukla Panchami), on Lalita Sahasranama Friday parayan days, on Purnima of every month (Tripura is honoured on full-moon days), and during the Navavarana puja of the Sri Chakra. Initiated Sri Vidya upasakas chant the Pancharatnam as the opening stuti before the Lalita Trishati or Lalita Sahasranama parayan. Daily morning recitation by Sri Vidya devotees, especially after the panchadashi or shodashi mantra japa, is the traditional practice. (Sources: Sri Vidya upasana paddhati; Drikpanchang Lalita festivals.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Tripura Sundari and the Sri Chakra?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Tripura Sundari is the indwelling deity (devata) of the Sri Chakra (also called Sri Yantra) — the supreme yantra of the Sri Vidya tradition consisting of nine interlocking triangles around a central bindu, surrounded by lotus petals and three protective walls. The Pancharatnam explicitly states Sri Chakra-raja-nilayam, bindu-madhya-nivasinim — she who dwells in the Sri Chakra-king and resides in the centre of the bindu. The Sri Chakra represents the entire cosmic manifestation in 43 triangles emerging from the central point; Tripura Sundari sits in the bindu as the source. The Shodashi mantra of sixteen syllables is her sound-form (shabda-rupa); the Sri Chakra is her geometric form (yantra-rupa); Lalita Mahatripura Sundari with red complexion, four arms holding pasha-ankusha-ikshu-pushpabana is her embodied form (svarupa). (Sources: Tripura Upanishad; Saubhagya Bhaskara commentary on Lalita Sahasranama.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconography of Tripura Sundari described in the Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pancharatnam's second verse describes Devi Tripura Sundari in classical Sri Vidya iconography: balarka-bimba-sankasham — radiant like the disc of the rising morning sun (sindura-red); chaturbahu-samanvitam — endowed with four arms; pasha-ankusha-dhanur-bana-dharinim — bearing the pasha (noose to bind the devotee in love), ankusha (goad to drive away ego), dhanur (sugarcane bow representing the mind) and bana (five flower-arrows representing the five tanmatras/sense-objects). She is youthful, supremely beautiful (lalita), seated on the lap of Kameshwara Shiva who reclines on the panchapreta-asana (the throne of the five corpse-deities Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra, Ishwara, Sadashiva). She wears red garments, gold ornaments, and a crown bearing the crescent moon. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama dhyana shloka; Soundarya Lahari verses 7-12.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Tripura Sundari Pancharatnam: (1) sarva-mangala-mangalyam — the supreme auspiciousness of all auspicious things (the goddess is herself the very source of mangala); (2) sarva-siddhi-pradayinim — the bestowal of all worldly and spiritual siddhis; (3) sarva-saubhagya-jananim — the granting of complete saubhagya (auspicious wifehood, marital harmony, beauty, longevity, prosperity); (4) success in marriage and removal of marital obstacles; (5) physical beauty, charm and magnetism (since she is sundari, beauty itself); (6) wealth, royal favour and political success (she is Rajarajeshwari, queen of queens); and (7) ultimately moksha through Sri Vidya upasana, since Tripura is the supreme Brahman in feminine form. The Pancharatnam is the daily prelude to Lalita Sahasranama parayan. (Source: Sri Vidya upasana phalashruti.)
